steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to make a generalization about circumnavigations. A generalization states a broad pattern drawn from the notes as a whole — not just a definition, not just a list of examples. The notes establish two pieces of information that together form a pattern: most circumnavigations include stops on land (note 2), but both Moreau and Kowalska completed nonstop circumnavigations (notes 4 and 6). The generalization must capture this contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Kowalska sailed FanFan around the world, while Moreau sailed Roxy.": Lists two examples with no broader claim — not a generalization. ✗
  • "Kowalska and Moreau have both embarked on sailing journeys around the world, also called circumnavigations.": Still just listing the two sailors; the parenthetical is a definition, not a generalization. ✗
  • "Most circumnavigations include stops on land, but some circumnavigations are nonstop.": States the general pattern from note 2 and contrasts it with the exceptions from notes 4 and 6. This synthesizes the notes into a true generalization. ✓
  • "A circumnavigation is defined as a sailing trip that goes in a complete circle around the world.": A definition, not a generalization; it ignores everything else in the notes. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
